message: bd-3on: legacy capture mock OpenAI server

 {"id":"bd-2qk","title":"Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity","description":"# Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature/functionality coverage of the original Pi Agent (TypeScript) with:
- Complete conformance harness validating behavioral equivalence
- Comprehensive test coverage without mocks where possible
- Full integration with sibling libraries (asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust)
- Detailed E2E integration test scripts with verbose logging

## Background
pi_agent_rust is a Rust port of Pi Agent (TypeScript CLI) by Mario Zechner. The port uses
spec-first methodology: extract behavior from legacy → implement from spec → never translate
line-by-line. Currently at ~85% feature complete with all core functionality working.

## Current State (as of 2026-02-02)
- ✅ Core Types: All message types, content blocks, usage tracking
- ✅ 7 Built-in Tools: read, bash, edit, write, grep, find, ls (122 conformance tests)
- ✅ Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Azure (all streaming + tool use)
- ✅ Agent Runtime: Full loop with tool iteration and event callbacks
- ✅ Session Management: JSONL v3 format, tree structure, branching
- ✅ Interactive TUI: Elm Architecture with bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour
- ✅ CLI: Full argument parsing, subcommands, package management
- ✅ Authentication: auth.json storage, OAuth login/logout
- ✅ Compaction: Full context compaction with LLM summarization
- ✅ RPC Mode: Complete JSON protocol implementation
- ✅ Benchmarks: Truncation, SSE parsing with performance budgets

## Key Gaps Requiring Work
1. **HTTP Migration**: Providers still use reqwest; asupersync HTTP client stubbed
2. **Extensions Runtime**: Protocol defined but no WASM loader/runtime
3. **Session Index Integration**: Implemented but not wired into CLI
4. **Themes**: Not implemented
5. **Test Coverage**: Critical gaps in provider streaming, TUI, CLI, compaction

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All providers use asupersync HTTP client (tokio removed)
- [ ] Extension runtime loads and executes WASM extensions
- [ ] Session picker uses SQLite index for fast lookups
- [ ] Theme system loads and applies themes
- [ ] 95%+ test coverage without mocks for core paths
- [ ] E2E tests with real API calls (recorded/VCR style)
- [ ] All conformance fixtures pass
- [ ] Performance targets met (startup <100ms, binary <20MB)

## Workstreams (child features)
1. HTTP Migration (HIGH priority) - Blocks tokio removal
2. Test Coverage Expansion (HIGH priority) - Quality gate
3. Session Index Integration (MEDIUM priority) - UX improvement
4. Themes (LOW priority) - Nice to have
5. Extensions Runtime (tracked separately in existing beads bd-25q)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:31:24.363150202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:31:24.363150202Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2tf","title":"Unit tests: extension discovery + install resolution","description":"Background:
- Discovery/install is user-facing and failure-prone; tests prevent regressions.

Steps:
- Unit tests for package source resolution (global vs project precedence).
- Tests for CLI flags (--extension/--no-extensions) and conflict handling.
- Validate diagnostics for missing/invalid extensions (actionable errors).
- Cover path normalization, symlink handling, and manifest discovery.

Logging requirements:
- Tests assert diagnostics include source, resolution path, and reason codes.

Acceptance:
- All discovery/install branches have unit coverage with deterministic outputs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:04:58.361767846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:37.400281159Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tf","depends_on_id":"bd-1e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:32.727103737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tf","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:37.400250952Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2tp","title":"Unit tests: extc pipeline + compat scanner","description":"Background:
- The extc build pipeline is the JS/TS compatibility backbone; correctness is critical.

Steps:
- Unit tests for JS/TS bundling with deterministic output.
- Verify shim rewrite passes (legacy API -> connector hostcalls).
- Compat scan tests: reject Node/Bun globals and forbidden APIs.
- Cache tests: identical inputs hit cache; changes bust cache.

Logging requirements:
- Tests assert diagnostics include rejected API name + location.

Acceptance:
- Pipeline behavior is deterministic and rejects incompatible code with actionable logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:05.088900030Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:47.667473657Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tp","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:47.667444432Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tp","depends_on_id":"bd-xgo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:42.547370766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2tx","title":"No-mock policy enforcement (lint + CI guard)","description":"Goal:
- Enforce the no-mock policy by adding a lightweight CI guard that flags new mock/fake usage in tests, with actionable logging.

Scope:
- Define explicit allowlist rules (file + rationale) for any exceptional mocks.
- Add a fast check (rg/ast-grep) that fails CI when Mock/Fake/Stub patterns appear outside allowlist.
- Emit clear diagnostics (file:line + matched symbol) and guidance to replace with VCR/real deps.
- Document the policy and how to request/justify exceptions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- CI fails if new mocks/fakes are introduced without allowlist entry.
- Output includes exact file:line matches and remediation guidance.
- Allowlist is minimal, audited, and documented.
- Guard is fast, deterministic, and runs in CI + locally.

Dependencies:
- Leverages bd-351 audit to seed allowlist and baseline mock inventory.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:27.589230634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:27:00.277654862Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tx","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:27.597014240Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tx","depends_on_id":"bd-351","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:43.626768288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2uv","title":"Define popularity criteria + target sample size","description":"Background:
- We must justify *why* each extension is in the sample and ensure diversity (not just what is easy to test).

What to decide (document explicitly):
- Popularity signals (e.g., GitHub stars, npm downloads, community references, official pi docs).
- Diversity axes (tool-only vs slash commands, event hooks, UI integrations, network usage, file system usage).
- Minimum and maximum sample size, plus rationale (coverage vs time).

Output:
- A written criteria section that later tasks can apply mechanically.

Acceptance:
- Criteria are precise enough that two people would pick the same sample.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:45.780340292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:02.804375173Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:02.804313057Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic sampling criteria + target size in CONFORMANCE.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2wo","title":"Define license/redistribution policy for extension artifacts + fixtures","description":"Background:
- We will archive extension artifacts and include fixtures. Licensing constraints must be honored.

Decisions to document:
- Which licenses allow redistribution of artifacts and fixture snippets.
- When to store hashes only vs full artifacts.
- Procedure for excluded/opt-out extensions.

Output:
- A policy note that maps license types to allowed storage actions.

Acceptance:
- Every sampled extension can be classified as OK / restricted / excluded under this policy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:21:27.469438600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:45:26.883830816Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:45:26.883767769Z","close_reason":"Documented license/redistribution policy for extension artifacts + fixtures in CONFORMANCE.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2x7","title":"E2E interactive smoke (tmux) + detailed logs","description":"Goal:
- Add a scripted E2E smoke test for interactive mode using tmux (per AGENTS.md), with detailed logs and captured panes.

Scope:
- Launch pi in tmux with fixed dimensions (80x24).
- Send a prompt, capture output, and verify key UI events.
- Capture logs/pane snapshots as test artifacts.
- Validate clean shutdown and no orphaned processes.

Logging Requirements:
- Use CLI E2E harness (bd-1wc) + TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log tmux commands, timings, pane captures, VCR_MODE, and cassette name/path.
- Save pane snapshots + logs on failure for debugging.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Deterministic run using VCR playback and stable terminal dimensions.
- Logs include tmux commands, pane captures, and assertions.
- Tests pass on CI environments that support tmux (skip with clear log if unavailable).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:18.073043109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:00.879928578Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x7","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:00:43.457111939Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:00:38.605726695Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x7","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:18.080271631Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x7","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:00.879901318Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:01:06.669769764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
